abstract: Porous heterogeneous catalysts are cornerstones of modern industrial chemistry. In this work, we report the synthesis and application of Hf-NU-1000, a novel hafnium-based metal-organic framework featuring Hf6 nodes. This material exhibits exceptional catalytic activity in the activation of epoxides, enabling the quantitative chemical fixation of CO2 into five-membered cyclic carbonates under ambient conditions. Beyond carbon capture, Hf-NU-1000 serves as a versatile multifunctional catalyst for a variety of epoxide transformations. Specifically, it facilitates the regioselective and enantioretentive formation of 1,2-bifunctionalized systems through solvolytic nucleophilic ring-opening reactions, highlighting its potential for precise organic synthesis.
